GMAIL-01.title: Email sender guidelines
GMAIL-01.supports: Authentication, TLS/PTR expectations, spam-rate guidance, subscription/unsubscribe requirements, and sender practices.

GMAIL-02.title: Email sender guidelines FAQ
GMAIL-02.supports: Daily spam-rate calculation; target below 0.1% and avoid 0.3% or higher.

GMAIL-03.title: Postmaster Tools
GMAIL-03.supports: Monitor Gmail delivery errors, spam reports, reputation, and related diagnostics.

YAHOO-01.title: Sender Best Practices
YAHOO-01.supports: SPF, DKIM, DMARC alignment, easy unsubscribe, complaint control, and list practices.

CRTC-CASL-01.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Canada's Anti-Spam Legislation
CRTC-CASL-01.supports: CASL CEMs require consent, identification information, and an unsubscribe mechanism; CEMs sent using a computer system in Canada generally remain subject to CASL when sent abroad, subject to limited exemptions.

CRTC-CASL-02.title: CASL Guidance on Implied Consent
CRTC-CASL-02.supports: CASL distinguishes express and implied consent; senders must be able to substantiate the consent they rely on.

EU-EC-01.title: Legal grounds for processing data
EU-EC-01.supports: GDPR processing requires an applicable legal ground; direct-marketing processing must be assessed under EU data-protection law.

EU-EC-02.title: When can personal data be processed?
EU-EC-02.supports: Legitimate-interest processing requires a case-specific assessment that the person's rights and freedoms do not override the interest.

FTC-01.title: CAN-SPAM Act: A Compliance Guide for Business
FTC-01.supports: Accurate headers, non-deceptive subjects, ad identification where required, postal address, and opt-out duties.

ICO-01.title: Electronic mail marketing
ICO-01.supports: Consent rules, soft opt-in, company-email treatment, and suppression expectations under PECR.

ICO-02.title: How do we comply with the PECR electronic mail marketing rules?
ICO-02.supports: Consent must be specific and informed for the channel and marketing purpose.

ICO-03.title: Business-to-business marketing
ICO-03.supports: B2B consent and lawful-basis nuances; corporate vs individual subscriber distinctions.

ICO-04.title: Using marketing lists
ICO-04.supports: Be transparent about marketing use and collect clear, channel-specific opt-in.

ICO-CONSENT-01.title: How should we obtain, record and manage consent?
ICO-CONSENT-01.supports: Keep a specific audit trail of who consented, when, what they were told, how they consented, and later withdrawal; retain the matching statement or form version.

HUB-CONSENT-01.title: Understand opt-in consent for email
HUB-CONSENT-01.supports: Explicit vs implied consent and practical opt-in mechanisms.

MC-PERM-01.title: Email Marketing Best Practices for Success
MC-PERM-01.supports: Permission-based marketing, double opt-in, and distinction from transactional mail.

MC-LISTS-01.title: Requirements and Best Practices for Audiences
MC-LISTS-01.supports: Prohibits purchased, rented, third-party, publicly available, scraped, and otherwise non-permission-based lists.

MC-SPAM-01.title: How Legitimate Marketers Can Prevent Spam Complaints
MC-SPAM-01.supports: Make unsubscribe prominent and reduce reasons recipients report spam.

MC-SEG-01.title: About Open and Click Rates
MC-SEG-01.supports: Segment by location, interests, or behavior to make content relevant.

MC-AB-01.title: Create an A/B Test
MC-AB-01.supports: Test one variable such as subject, sender, content, or send time, with controlled variations.

MC-TIME-01.title: Use Send Time Optimization
MC-TIME-01.supports: Use recipient engagement data to choose send time instead of assuming a universal best hour.

MC-SUBJ-01.title: Best Practices for Email Subject Lines
MC-SUBJ-01.supports: Descriptive, short subject lines; careful emoji use; subject-line testing.

MC-REPORT-01.title: Getting Started with Reports
MC-REPORT-01.supports: Delivery, opens, clicks, recipients, and report drill-down.

MC-CONV-01.title: About the Conversion Insights Dashboard
MC-CONV-01.supports: Revenue attribution, funnel performance, store visits, and sales.

MC-GA-01.title: Integrate Google Analytics with Mailchimp
MC-GA-01.supports: UTM/link tracking and post-click conversion measurement.

MC-GDPR-01.title: Collect Consent with GDPR Forms
MC-GDPR-01.supports: Explain why data is collected, offer separate affirmative choices for marketing activities, identify the collector, and retain the form version shown at signup.

MC-DOI-01.title: About Double Opt-in
MC-DOI-01.supports: Double opt-in adds a confirmation step; only after verification is a contact added as subscribed, and the method should be selected for the company’s needs or requirements.

MC-PROFILE-01.title: About Contact Profile Pages
MC-PROFILE-01.supports: Contact profiles expose signup origin, marketing state, opted-in date, permission timestamp, signup method, and the text version of a GDPR-enabled signup form.

LIT-ACCESS-01.title: The Ultimate Guide to Email Accessibility in 2026
LIT-ACCESS-01.supports: Live text, strong contrast, accessible templates, and ongoing accessibility practices.

LIT-QA-01.title: The Ultimate Email Checklist
LIT-QA-01.supports: Pre-send checks for rendering, plain text, personalization fallbacks, images, ALT text, and dark mode.

LIT-MOBILE-01.title: Why You Should Be Using Responsive Email Templates
LIT-MOBILE-01.supports: Responsive single-column layouts and sufficiently large tappable CTAs.

LIT-MPP-01.title: Email Analytics: How to Measure Email Marketing Success
LIT-MPP-01.supports: Open rate is unreliable alone because Apple Mail Privacy Protection obscures true opens.

LIT-TEST-01.title: How Email Content May be Killing Your Deliverability
LIT-TEST-01.supports: Validate links, rendering, dark mode, image sizes, accessibility, and seed tests before launch.

AWS-SES-SEP-01.title: Using configuration sets in Amazon SES
AWS-SES-SEP-01.supports: Configuration sets and dedicated IP pools can separate traffic classes such as transactional and marketing mail for distinct reputation management.

AWS-SES-MON-01.title: Monitoring your Amazon SES sending activity
AWS-SES-MON-01.supports: Monitor sends, deliveries, bounces, complaints, rejects, opens, clicks, and delivery delays through SES event publishing and metrics.

AWS-SES-FORMAT-01.title: Email format and Amazon SES
AWS-SES-FORMAT-01.supports: Multipart email can include both HTML and plain-text alternatives for client compatibility and accessibility.

BRAZE-STRAT-01.title: AI-powered Email Marketing Strategy for 2026
BRAZE-STRAT-01.supports: Lifecycle goals, dynamic segments, automation, deliverability, preference centers, and sunset policies.

BRAZE-CTA-01.title: Email Campaign Best Practices: A Guide
BRAZE-CTA-01.supports: Use a clear, actionable call to action and keep the message focused on the intended next step.

BRAZE-TRIG-01.title: What Is a Trigger Campaign?
BRAZE-TRIG-01.supports: Behavioral and time-based triggers increase relevance across lifecycle stages.

BRAZE-METRIC-01.title: Email Metrics That Matter in 2025
BRAZE-METRIC-01.supports: Align KPIs to goals, segment reporting by lifecycle stage, and use tests to validate changes.

BRAZE-TRANS-01.title: Transactional vs. Promotional Emails
BRAZE-TRANS-01.supports: Distinguishes essential service messages from commercial conversion-focused messages.

BRAZE-HOLIDAY-01.title: Holiday Email Deliverability Best Practices
BRAZE-HOLIDAY-01.supports: Avoid sudden volume/frequency escalation, offer frequency controls, and expand to less-engaged audiences cautiously.

KL-WELCOME-01.title: How to create an email welcome series
KL-WELCOME-01.supports: Immediate welcome, three-email baseline over one week, incentive delivery, story, products, and testing.

KL-CONSENT-01.title: How to collect GDPR-compliant consent
KL-CONSENT-01.supports: Use granular marketing choices and retain the form ID, capture method, form version, and exact language associated with consent.

KL-DOI-01.title: Understanding the double opt-in process
KL-DOI-01.supports: Double opt-in verifies a submitted address or number; only confirmed subscribers join the list and enter welcome, while unconfirmed submissions remain outside the subscribed list.

KL-FORMS-01.title: How to reduce spam complaint rates
KL-FORMS-01.supports: Signup forms should identify the contacted channels, data use, expected content, and preference or frequency choices; confirmation and double opt-in help align expectations.

KL-CART-01.title: How to create an abandoned cart flow
KL-CART-01.supports: First reminder 2–4 hours after checkout abandonment, second 20–48 hours later, and dynamic cart content.

KL-BROWSE-01.title: How to create a browse abandonment flow
KL-BROWSE-01.supports: Use a lighter touch than cart, require viewed-product tracking, exclude later funnel actions, and limit repeat entry.

KL-POST-01.title: How to create a post-purchase flow
KL-POST-01.supports: Post-purchase relationship building, review requests, cross-sell, and sequence examples.

KL-XSELL-01.title: How to create an upsell or cross-sell flow
KL-XSELL-01.supports: Use placed/fulfilled order triggers, category filters, relevant recommendations, and timing near product receipt.

KL-REPLEN-01.title: How to create a replenishment flow
KL-REPLEN-01.supports: Derive replenishment timing from actual product/customer buying cycles.

KL-WINBACK-01.title: How to create a winback flow
KL-WINBACK-01.supports: Re-engage past customers based on inactivity and past purchase behavior.

KL-SUNSET-01.title: How to create a sunset flow
KL-SUNSET-01.supports: Final re-engagement attempt followed by suppression of unresponsive contacts.

KL-NEVER-01.title: How to create a never engaged segment
KL-NEVER-01.supports: Identify and avoid repeatedly mailing never-engaged contacts; consider final attempt or suppression.

KL-PRICE-01.title: How to create a price drop flow
KL-PRICE-01.supports: Trigger price-drop messages from viewed/checkout behavior and product price changes.

KL-STOCK-01.title: How to build a back in stock flow
KL-STOCK-01.supports: Collect explicit back-in-stock interest and send when inventory returns.

KL-LOWSTOCK-01.title: How to create a low inventory flow
KL-LOWSTOCK-01.supports: Send immediately on inventory threshold, exclude recent purchasers, and avoid unnecessary repeated alerts.

KL-VIP-01.title: How to create a segment of VIP customers
KL-VIP-01.supports: Engage VIPs with early access, exclusive content, personalized bundles, and surveys.

KL-SEGTEST-01.title: Advanced segmentation reference
KL-SEGTEST-01.supports: Use sampled/control segments to validate campaign impact.

KL-HOLDOUT-01.title: Getting started with global holdout groups
KL-HOLDOUT-01.supports: Global holdouts exclude profiles across campaign and flow channels for a defined test window, retain membership history, and allow essential transactional messages.

HUB-WF-01.title: Create workflows
HUB-WF-01.supports: Enrollment triggers, filters, actions, re-enrollment, and unenrollment for automated campaigns.

HUB-AUTO-01.title: Automate your processes
HUB-AUTO-01.supports: Segment and target high-quality leads before automation.

HUB-AB-01.title: Run A/B tests for marketing emails
HUB-AB-01.supports: Sample-based A/B testing, winning metrics, test duration, and fallback version.

HUB-B2B-01.title: B2B Email Marketing
HUB-B2B-01.supports: Segment by industry/role/behavior and measure pipeline sourced, influenced, and velocity.

HUB-LAUNCH-01.title: How to Create a Product Launch Email
HUB-LAUNCH-01.supports: Launch-email structure and multi-message product-launch sequence.

HUB-EVENT-01.title: Connect HubSpot and Zoom
HUB-EVENT-01.supports: Segment registrants, attendees, and no-shows; automate post-event actions.

HUB-CAL-01.title: Insert calendar invites into marketing emails
HUB-CAL-01.supports: Include Google Calendar and ICS options for broad calendar compatibility.

MC-NEWS-01.title: How to Write the Best Email Newsletter
MC-NEWS-01.supports: Clear header, subject, audience-tailored content, concise structure, and visual interest.

MC-NEWS-02.title: How to Create an Impactful Email Newsletter Format
MC-NEWS-02.supports: Scannable hierarchy, short paragraphs, headings, and layout testing.

MC-EVENT-01.title: Optimize Your Event Email Marketing
MC-EVENT-01.supports: Event reminder cadence of roughly two weeks, one week, and one day, plus access/troubleshooting details.

MC-EVENT-02.title: Timing Your Event Reminder Emails
MC-EVENT-02.supports: Clear actionable subjects, essential logistics, concise content, and mobile optimization.

ZOOM-EVENT-01.title: Zoom Webinar Checklist
ZOOM-EVENT-01.supports: Send attendee/registrant follow-up with recording and a next-step CTA.

MC-RENEW-01.title: Best Practices for Renewal Email Campaigns
MC-RENEW-01.supports: Renewal sequence beginning 45–60 days before expiry, then 30 days, 7–10 days, and 1–3 days.

MC-REF-01.title: Referral Email Examples, Templates & Tips
MC-REF-01.supports: Segment referral asks, automate prompts, coordinate timing, and track referral links.

MC-REF-02.title: How to Maximize Referral Sources for Higher Conversions
MC-REF-02.supports: Track referral codes, actual sales, customer value, and source quality.

MC-FOLLOW-01.title: Automated Email Follow-Ups Boost Engagement and Action
MC-FOLLOW-01.supports: Use a prompt follow-up, value-added messages, and a clear final next step; generally 2–5 follow-ups depending on context.

MC-RESEND-01.title: Resend an Unopened Email
MC-RESEND-01.supports: Resending can increase unsubscribes and complaints, especially when repeated; use cautiously.

AWS-SES-WARM-01.title: Warming up dedicated IP addresses (standard)
AWS-SES-WARM-01.supports: New sending domains/IPs need gradual volume warm-up (reputation-building time varies by provider); automatic SES warm-up ramps over 45 days; send to engaged recipients first and avoid a sudden large volume increase after warm-up completes.

GOOGLE-DMARC-01.title: Set up DMARC
GOOGLE-DMARC-01.supports: DMARC record location is a `_dmarc.<domain>` TXT record with a required `p=` policy tag (none/quarantine/reject); set up SPF and DKIM first; a message passes DMARC via SPF authentication+alignment or DKIM authentication+alignment.

IETF-RFC8058-01.title: RFC 8058: Signaling One-Click Functionality for List Email Headers
IETF-RFC8058-01.supports: Defines one-click unsubscribe: an HTTPS `List-Unsubscribe` URI plus `List-Unsubscribe-Post: List-Unsubscribe=One-Click`, processed by a single POST with no confirmation page.

MS-SNDS-01.title: Outlook.com postmaster / IP and domain management
MS-SNDS-01.supports: Since May 5, 2025, Outlook.com enforces SPF/DKIM/DMARC authentication for domains sending 5,000+ messages per day; unauthenticated qualifying mail is junked, with rejection planned for continued non-compliance.
